task_completion.description: Verify the message recipient is set to Dr. Philip Ho.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Philip Ho' or 'Dr. Philip Ho' in the 'To' or 'Recipient' field of the internal message composer.

task_completion.description: Verify the message is linked to the patient record for Rosa Fritsch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Rosa Fritsch', 'PID 4', or '1952-04-04' in the 'Patient', 'Regarding', or 'Link' field of the message composer, indicating the patient context is attached.

task_completion.description: Verify the subject line is correctly filled out.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact text 'Elevated BP Alert - Rosa Fritsch' in the 'Subject' field of the message.

task_completion.description: Verify the message body contains the required BP reading and review request.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text '158/94' (or '158/94 mmHg') and words indicating a request for provider review in the main message text area.

task_completion.description: Verify the message was successfully sent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a success confirmation toast/alert (e.g., 'Message Sent'), or the message appearing in the user's 'Sent' messages folder.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual application GUI to send the message.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the EHR/medical system's web interface being interacted with, not terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the message was composed in the system's internal messaging interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text fields should be part of the application's message composition window, not a generic text editor like Notepad.

integrity.description: Verify the success state appears genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The success confirmation or sent folder should look like standard application behavior without signs of browser developer tools (Inspect Element) being open or used to fake the result.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Rosa Fritsch (PID: 4), DOB: 1952-04-04. Recipient: Philip Ho. Blood Pressure: 158/94. Expected Subject: 'Elevated BP Alert - Rosa Fritsch'.
